questions.question: Explain the difference between the connective 'and' (∧) and 'or' (∨) in a logical statement. Provide a truth table to support your answer.
What is the truth value of the statement p → q when p is true and q is false? Show the relevant row in the truth table for the implication connective.
Construct the truth table for the statement (p ∧ q) ∨ r. What is the truth value of this statement when p is false, q is true, and r is false? Use the truth table to justify your answer.
